QF.id: I Codes of Conduct and Investor Protection_30

question.text: Under SEBI's ODR framework, within how many calendar days must a Market Participant submit its intention to challenge an arbitral award under Section 34 of the Arbitration Act in the ODR Portal?

answer.text: 7 calendar days of the issuance/pronouncement of the arbitral award

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The text states: "the party against whom order has been passed, will be required to submit its intention to challenge the award under Section 34 of the Arbitration Act within 7 calendar days in the ODR Portal for onward notification to the party/ies in whose favour the arbitral award has been passed and the relevant MII." **The Trap:** 30 calendar days is the general period for arbitrators to pass the arbitral award — not the challenge intention timeline. 7 days is specifically for declaring intent to challenge. **Cross-Exam Context:** The 3-month rule: if a stay is not granted within 3 months of receipt of award, complete adherence to award terms is required — even during the challenge. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** 7 days = declare challenge intention; 15 days = market participant must pay award amount (absent stay); 3 months = if no stay, must comply. Three key timeframes.
